meta.description: Every professional boxing title belt across all five bodies and all nine pro weight classes. World title belts are the highest achievement in pro boxing per sanctioning body. The Ring Magazine lineal belt represents the true champion — the fighter who beat the man who beat the man. Multiple world titles can be unified — a unified champion holds belts from multiple bodies simultaneously. The engine tracks holder, defences, and mandatory challenger status per belt. currentHolderId is null at start — populated during world generation.

belts.label: WBC World Flyweight Championship
belts.description: The WBC's flagship flyweight title. Green and gold — one of boxing's four major world championships at 50.8kg. A mandatory challenger from rank 1 must be fought within 52 weeks.

belts.label: WBC World Bantamweight Championship
belts.description: The WBC's flagship bantamweight title at 53.5kg. One of boxing's four major world championships. A mandatory challenger must be fought within 52 weeks.

belts.label: WBC World Featherweight Championship
belts.description: The WBC's flagship featherweight title at 57.2kg. One of boxing's four major world championships. A mandatory challenger must be fought within 52 weeks.

belts.label: WBC World Lightweight Championship
belts.description: The WBC's flagship lightweight title at 61.2kg. One of boxing's four major world championships and historically one of the most competitive divisions.

belts.label: WBC World Welterweight Championship
belts.description: The WBC's flagship welterweight title at 66.7kg. One of the sport's most storied world championships and a perennial showcase division for marquee fights.

belts.label: WBC World Middleweight Championship
belts.description: The WBC's flagship middleweight title at 72.6kg. One of boxing's four major world championships and a division that has produced some of the sport's most iconic champions.

belts.label: WBC World Light Heavyweight Championship
belts.description: The WBC's flagship light heavyweight title at 79.4kg. One of boxing's major world championships. A division known for punching power and dramatic finishes.

belts.label: WBC World Cruiserweight Championship
belts.description: The WBC's flagship cruiserweight title at 90.7kg. A world championship and frequent stepping stone for fighters on the path to heavyweight glory.

belts.label: WBC World Heavyweight Championship
belts.description: The most coveted belt in the WBC — the heavyweight championship of the world. No weight limit. The linchpin of every undisputed heavyweight conversation and the belt that defines eras.

belts.label: WBC Silver Flyweight Title
belts.description: The WBC's secondary flyweight title. Silver champions are the mandatory next challenger to the WBC World title — winning this belt puts a fighter one fight away from world championship level.

belts.label: WBC Silver Bantamweight Title
belts.description: The WBC's secondary bantamweight title. Silver champions are the mandatory next challenger to the WBC World title at bantamweight.

belts.label: WBC Silver Featherweight Title
belts.description: The WBC's secondary featherweight title. Silver champions are the mandatory next challenger to the WBC World title at featherweight.

belts.label: WBC Silver Lightweight Title
belts.description: The WBC's secondary lightweight title. Silver champions are the mandatory next challenger to the WBC World title at lightweight.

belts.label: WBC Silver Welterweight Title
belts.description: The WBC's secondary welterweight title. Silver champions are the mandatory next challenger to the WBC World title at welterweight.

belts.label: WBC Silver Middleweight Title
belts.description: The WBC's secondary middleweight title. Silver champions are the mandatory next challenger to the WBC World title at middleweight.

belts.label: WBC Silver Light Heavyweight Title
belts.description: The WBC's secondary light heavyweight title. Silver champions are the mandatory next challenger to the WBC World title at light heavyweight.

belts.label: WBC Silver Cruiserweight Title
belts.description: The WBC's secondary cruiserweight title. Silver champions are the mandatory next challenger to the WBC World title at cruiserweight.

belts.label: WBC Silver Heavyweight Title
belts.description: The WBC's secondary heavyweight title. Silver champions are the mandatory next challenger to the WBC World Heavyweight Championship.

belts.label: WBA Super World Flyweight Championship
belts.description: The WBA's highest flyweight designation. Awarded when a champion unifies the WBA title with other major belts, or recognised as the premier WBA flyweight champion. Supersedes the WBA World title when both are active.

belts.label: Ring Magazine Lineal Flyweight Championship
belts.description: The lineal flyweight championship — the truest measure of who rules the flyweight division. Passed only by defeat in the ring. The fighter who holds this title beat the man who beat the man. Cannot be stripped, only lost.

belts.label: Ring Magazine Lineal Bantamweight Championship
belts.description: The lineal bantamweight championship. Passed only by defeat in the ring. The fighter who holds this title beat the man who beat the man. Cannot be stripped, only lost.

belts.label: Ring Magazine Lineal Welterweight Championship
belts.description: The lineal welterweight championship — one of boxing's most celebrated titles. Passed only by defeat in the ring. The fighter who holds this title beat the man who beat the man.

belts.description: The lineal middleweight championship. Passed only by defeat in the ring. The fighter who holds this title beat the man who beat the man. Cannot be stripped, only lost.

belts.label: Ring Magazine Lineal Light Heavyweight Championship
belts.description: The lineal light heavyweight championship. Passed only by defeat in the ring. The fighter who holds this title beat the man who beat the man. Cannot be stripped, only lost.

belts.description: The lineal cruiserweight championship. Passed only by defeat in the ring. The fighter who holds this title beat the man who beat the man. Cannot be stripped, only lost.

belts.label: Ring Magazine Lineal Heavyweight Championship
belts.description: The most coveted title in all of boxing. The lineal heavyweight championship of the world — passed only by defeat in the ring since the beginning of the sport. The fighter who holds this belt is, without argument, the best heavyweight alive. It cannot be stripped, only lost.
